destinations.evidence: Incident buffer defaults, hard cap, and calldata bound are explicit.
Tracing is enabled by default unless disabled and stored calldata is truncated.
Writer is bounded, evicts oldest entries, and drops on contention with a counter instead of blocking.
Reader returns newest-first incidents capped by configured capacity.

equivalence_argument: Exact mapping in ritual-metrics scheduled incident buffer.
rationale: The non-blocking try_lock rule is incorporated into the later bounded incident buffer invariant with capacity, truncation, eviction, enablement, and contention-drop semantics.
statement: Scheduled incident collection is enabled by default unless RITUAL_SCHEDULED_INCIDENT_TRACE_ENABLED=0, uses a bounded ring buffer with default capacity 1024 and hard cap 50000, truncates stored calldata to 2048 bytes, evicts oldest entries when full, and drops samples with a contention counter instead of blocking payload building when the buffer lock is contended.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set incident trace capacity and record more entries than capacity, then assert newest-first retrieval and oldest-entry eviction.
Hold the incident buffer lock while recording another incident and assert the call returns promptly, appends nothing, and increments scheduled_incident_dropped_on_contention.
Disable tracing with RITUAL_SCHEDULED_INCIDENT_TRACE_ENABLED=0 and assert no incidents are stored.
summary: Scheduled incident buffer is bounded and best-effort in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Capacity=2, record A/B/C and assert C,B newest-first; hold lock and assert a dropped-on-contention increment; disable env and assert no storage.
title: Scheduled incident buffer is bounded and best-effort
